items.title: Merging & Rebasing
items.description: Once you have branches you need to combine them — merge preserves history, rebase rewrites it.

items.title: Conflict Resolution
items.description: Merging branches that touched the same lines produces conflicts; resolving them is its own skill.

items.title: Git Workflows
items.description: Trunk-based, GitHub Flow and GitFlow are all opinionated ways to use commits and branches.

items.title: Pull Requests & Reviews
items.description: PRs are the unit of review and merge — a branch + commits packaged for a teammate to inspect.

items.title: Reusable Modules & Packaging
items.description: Disciplined commits keep `setup.py` / `pyproject.toml` changes reviewable and bisectable.
